sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Union Pacific Guarantees Post-Merger Jobs to American Trade Dispatchers Union
feed.summary: This article briefly reports on Union Pacific's guarantee of post-merger jobs to the American Trade Dispatchers Union. It also includes information on Unity Software's fourth-quarter financial results, noting a 35% revenue increase year-over-year to $609 million and a quarterly loss of 66 cents per share. The content implies that the full report from WSJ requires logging in.

feed.title: U.S. Nuclear Regulatory Commission Approves License Renewal Application for Extended Operations of Diablo Canyon
feed.summary: The U.S. Nuclear Regulatory Commission (NRC) has approved Pacific Gas and Electric Company's 20-year license renewal for the Diablo Canyon Power Plant, ensuring its continued operation to provide clean energy to millions of Californians. This approval follows a three-year review process and aligns with state mandates to keep the plant running beyond its original planned retirement to ensure grid reliability and meet growing energy demand. Diablo Canyon is California's largest source of clean energy, contributing significantly to the state's decarbonization efforts and providing substantial annual financial benefits from avoided greenhouse gas emissions.

feed.title: RSU grant boosts Atmus (NYSE: ATMU) CEO Disher’s equity stake
feed.summary: Atmus Filtration Technologies' CEO, Stephanie Disher, was granted 31,492 restricted stock units (RSUs) as part of the company's 2026 long-term incentive program. These RSUs will vest in three equal tranches starting March 1, 2027, increasing her direct holdings to 388,676 shares of common stock. This grant is a form of compensation aimed at aligning executive interests with shareholder value.

feed.title: SPG (SPG) assistant general counsel granted restricted stock, shares withheld for taxes
feed.summary: Simon Property Group Inc.'s assistant general counsel, Kevin M. Kelly, received an award of 1,361 shares of restricted stock. To cover tax withholding obligations upon vesting, 478 shares were withheld at a price of $186.53 per share. After these transactions, Kelly holds 22,731 shares directly and 104 shares indirectly through a 401(k) plan.

feed.title: Atmus (NYSE: ATMU) CFO receives 5,163 RSUs in 2026 stock grant
feed.summary: Atmus Filtration Technologies Inc. (NYSE: ATMU) Chief Financial Officer Jack Kienzler was granted 5,163 restricted stock units (RSUs) as part of the company's 2026 long-term incentive program. These RSUs were awarded at no cash cost and will vest in three equal tranches starting March 1, 2027, converting into shares of Atmus common stock. Following this grant, Kienzler now holds a total of 80,936 shares of Atmus common stock directly.

feed.title: Simon Property (NYSE: SPG) SVP granted stock; shares withheld for taxes
feed.summary: Matthew A. Jackson, SVP and assistant treasurer of Simon Property Group (NYSE: SPG), was granted 1,361 shares of restricted common stock under the company's 2019 Stock Incentive Plan. To cover tax obligations upon vesting, 447 shares were withheld at a price of $186.53 per share in a non-market transaction. Following these transactions, Jackson directly holds 9,255 shares of common stock.

feed.title: Great American Insurance Group Announces the Promotion of Jesse F. Gutierrez to Divisional President, Professional Liability
feed.summary: Great American Insurance Group has promoted Jesse F. Gutierrez to Divisional President, Professional Liability, effective April 27, 2026. He will succeed Reina L. Gregorio, who is retiring in July. Mr. Gutierrez joined the company in 2013 and has extensive leadership experience within the Professional Liability division.

feed.title: SPG (NYSE: SPG) EVP awarded restricted stock and has shares withheld for taxes
feed.summary: Donald G. Frey, EVP and Treasurer of Simon Property Group (SPG), was awarded 2,721 shares of restricted common stock on April 1, 2026. Concurrently, 1,312 shares were withheld at $186.53 each to cover tax obligations related to the vesting of restricted stock. Following these transactions, Frey directly holds 22,573 common shares and indirectly holds 196 shares in a 401(k) plan.

feed.title: Simon Property Group (NYSE: SPG) counsel reports 1,048-share tax withholding
feed.summary: Steven E. Fivel, General Counsel for Simon Property Group (NYSE: SPG), reported a tax-related share disposition rather than an open-market trade. On April 1, 2026, 1,048 shares of common stock were withheld at $186.53 per share to cover tax obligations from vesting restricted stock. After this transaction, Fivel directly owns 40,986 shares and indirectly holds an additional 383 shares through his spouse.

feed.title: SPG (SPG) CEO Eli Simon has 2,030 shares withheld for taxes on vesting
feed.summary: Eli Simon, CEO of Simon Property Group Inc. (SPG), reported a routine tax-related disposition where 2,030 shares of common stock were withheld to cover tax obligations upon the vesting of restricted stock. The shares were valued at $186.53 each. Following this transaction, Simon directly holds 56,455 shares of SPG common stock.

feed.title: AMN Healthcare Services, DaVita, Bristol-Myers Squibb, Novavax, and AbbVie Shares Plummet, What You Need To Know
feed.summary: Shares of several healthcare and pharmaceutical companies, including AMN Healthcare Services, DaVita, Bristol-Myers Squibb, Novavax, and AbbVie, plummeted following reports that the U.S. might impose up to 100% tariffs on imported branded and patented drugs. This potential move, targeting companies not negotiating lower drug prices, caused a significant sell-off in the sector. Novavax's shares were particularly volatile, though the market's reaction suggests the news is meaningful but not fundamentally business-altering.

feed.title: Roper Technologies (NASDAQ:ROP) Could Be A Buy For Its Upcoming Dividend
feed.summary: Roper Technologies (NASDAQ:ROP) is set to pay a dividend of US$0.91 per share, with the ex-dividend date around April 6th, and a strong track record of dividend growth. The company demonstrates excellent dividend sustainability, with payout ratios of just 24% of profit and 15% of free cash flow, alongside robust earnings per share growth of 18% per annum over the past five years. This financial health suggests Roper Technologies is a promising dividend stock due to its reinvestment focus and consistent dividend increases.

feed.title: Packaging Corp. of America to lay off 110 tied to Chesterfield facility
feed.summary: Packaging Corp. of America (PCA) is set to lay off 110 employees with the closure of its Chesterfield County converting operation, with most job terminations expected by early June. The company cited a "difficult business decision" and plans to offer support, including assistance with transfers to other PCA locations. This follows other closures and layoffs reported in late 2025 across various PCA facilities.

feed.title: GDIT to lay off 95 employees across two offices in Montgomery County
feed.summary: General Dynamics IT (GDIT) is set to lay off 95 employees across two offices in Montgomery County, marking the company's second round of layoffs in recent months. GDIT is a prominent federal contractor in the Greater Washington region. This news follows a tumultuous year for several companies in the area, including Capital One and Amazon, which have also announced significant job cuts.

feed.title: Crown Castle Inc Stock (CCI) Moved Up by 7.14% on Apr 2: What Signal Does It Send?
feed.summary: Crown Castle Inc. (CCI) stock rose by 7.14% following the announcement of a comprehensive restructuring plan. This plan includes divesting small cell and fiber operations, a $7.00 billion debt reduction, and a $1.00 billion share buyback, aiming to create a more focused tower business and bolster investor confidence. Despite previous concerns about organic growth and valuation, these strategic initiatives are seen as positive catalysts for the company's financial future.

feed.title: Vegas hotel bundle from $200 adds meals, drinks, High Roller
feed.summary: Caesars Entertainment has launched an "Inclusive Summer Package" for Harrah's Las Vegas, The LINQ Hotel, and Flamingo Las Vegas, starting at $200 per night. The package includes accommodations, taxes, resort fees, two meals daily, bottomless drinks, High Roller Observation Wheel tickets, and free self-parking. This initiative aims to provide value options for travelers visiting the Las Vegas Strip from April through August 2026.

feed.title: General Motors (GM): One of the Most Undervalued Value Stocks to Buy Right Now
feed.summary: General Motors Company (NYSE: GM) is considered one of the most undervalued value stocks, with 66% of analysts maintaining bullish ratings and a consensus price target implying a 36.52% upside. The company plans to increase production of heavy-duty Silverado and Sierra trucks due to strong demand for their gas-powered truck franchise, despite rising gasoline prices. While GM is seen as a promising investment, the article suggests that some AI stocks may offer even higher and quicker returns.

feed.title: Is Your Town on the List? Customers in Hundreds of Minnesota Communities Getting Electricity Refunds
feed.summary: Hundreds of Minnesota communities are set to receive electricity refunds this month, stemming from the 2022 Inflation Reduction Act and lower fuel costs. Xcel Energy customers in 612 Minnesota communities will see a direct reduction in their electric bills, with an average household receiving $2.46 off their monthly bill for nine months, plus an additional $1.11 monthly due to lower fuel costs. The article provides a comprehensive list of qualifying communities.

feed.title: Hims & Hers Health, Regeneron, Biogen, BioMarin Pharmaceutical, and Moderna Shares Are Falling, What You Need To Know
feed.summary: Shares of Hims & Hers Health, Regeneron, Biogen, BioMarin Pharmaceutical, and Moderna fell after reports suggested the U.S. might impose up to 100% tariffs on imported branded and patented drugs. This potential move targets pharmaceutical companies that have not agreed to lower drug prices in the United States, prompting a significant sell-off in the sector. The article notes that stock market overreactions can create buying opportunities for high-quality stocks.

feed.title: [Form 4] HUNT J B TRANSPORT SERVICES INC Insider Trading Activity
feed.summary: Shelley Simpson, President and CEO of HUNT J B TRANSPORT SERVICES INC (JBHT), reported stock activity related to compensation. A performance-based restricted stock unit award partially vested on March 31, 2026, resulting in 3,954 RSUs converting to common stock, while 1,456 RSUs were forfeited. Additionally, 1,754 common shares were withheld to cover tax obligations, and Simpson now holds 83,623 common shares directly, along with other direct and indirect holdings.

feed.title: [Form 4] HUNT J B TRANSPORT SERVICES INC Insider Trading Activity
feed.summary: H.B. Hunt Transport Services Chairman John N. Roberts settled a performance-based Restricted Stock Unit (RSU) award, converting 7,316 RSUs into common shares and forfeiting 2,693 RSUs due to partial vesting. To cover tax obligations, 172 common shares were withheld. Following these transactions, Roberts directly holds 335,241 shares of common stock, in addition to shares in a 401(k) account.

feed.title: Trillium urges NextEra (NYSE: NEE) shareholders to back Paris-alignment report at May 21 meeting
feed.summary: Trillium Asset Management is urging NextEra Energy (NYSE: NEE) shareholders to vote FOR Proposal 4 at the May 21, 2026, annual meeting. This proposal requests a report detailing how NextEra plans to align its operations and investments with the Paris Agreement, especially after NextEra dropped its prior zero-emissions-by-2045 target. Trillium highlights NextEra's significant physical asset exposure to climate risks and argues that a clear climate transition plan is crucial for investors and the company's long-term competitive position.

feed.title: JB Hunt (NASDAQ: JBHT) EVP converts 420 RSUs into common stock
feed.summary: Spencer Frazier, EVP of Sales and Marketing at J.B. Hunt Transport Services (NASDAQ: JBHT), exercised 575 performance-based restricted stock units (RSUs) on March 31, 2026. Of these, 420 RSUs vested and were converted into common stock, while 155 were forfeited due to performance relative to target levels. Following the transaction, Frazier's holdings include 6,603.7668 shares directly, 1,730.9352 shares indirectly through his spouse, and 12,567.4414 shares in a 401(k) plan.

feed.title: Letter to the editor: The University of Iowa must end their relationship with Aramark
feed.summary: A letter to the editor advocates for the University of Iowa to terminate its contract with Aramark, citing three primary concerns. The author highlights Aramark's involvement with ICE detention centers, its history of serving poor-quality food in prisons, and its use of incarcerated individuals as unpaid "students" through the IN2WORK program. The Young Democratic Socialists of America at the University of Iowa are calling for this disassociation due to these ethical issues.

feed.title: Bullish Analyst Sentiment on Adobe (ADBE) Amid Ongoing AI Advancements
feed.summary: Analyst sentiment for Adobe (ADBE) is mixed despite a consensus price target implying a 32.00% upside. While Adobe announced a 12% rise in revenue and operating profitability, a leadership change with CEO Shantanu Narayan's retirement led Argus to downgrade the stock to Hold and Goldman Sachs analyst Gabriela Borges to reiterate a Sell rating. Despite ongoing AI advancements in Adobe's products, Wall Street remains wary.

feed.title: Lamb Weston Analysts Cut Their Forecasts After Q3 Results
feed.summary: Lamb Weston Holdings reported stronger-than-expected Q3 earnings and revenue, beating analyst estimates, and raised its 2026 sales guidance. Despite the positive financial performance, several analysts adjusted their price targets for the company downwards. CEO Mike Smith highlighted the company's focus on operational rigor and cost discipline as key drivers.

feed.title: Raymond James Financial Issues Pessimistic Forecast for Boyd Group Services (TSE:BYD) Stock Price
feed.summary: Raymond James Financial has lowered its price target for Boyd Group Services (TSE:BYD) from C$275 to C$270, though this still implies a significant 59% upside from its current trading price. Other analysts have also trimmed their targets, yet the consensus rating remains a "Buy" with an average target of C$263.00. The stock recently traded down to C$169.35 on heavy volume, significantly below its moving averages, and an insider, Brian Kaner, recently increased his stake by purchasing 1,180 shares.

feed.title: Synopsys Data Breach Lawsuit Investigation
feed.summary: Shamis & Gentile P.A. is investigating a data breach affecting Synopsys, Inc. that exposed sensitive personally identifiable information due to a ransomware attack by Arkana Security. The breach, which occurred on June 6, 2025, and was disclosed to the Massachusetts Attorney General on April 2, 2026, involved the theft of various types of personal data including driver's licenses, financial information, and social security numbers. Individuals whose data was compromised may be eligible for compensation and are encouraged to submit a claim.

feed.title: Duke Energy (NYSE: DUK) EVP uses 38 shares to cover RSU tax
feed.summary: Duke Energy EVP Scott L. Batson had 38 shares of common stock withheld to cover taxes on the vesting of 87 restricted stock units (RSUs). The transaction occurred on April 1, 2026, with shares valued at $130.90 each. Following this tax-related withholding, Mr. Batson directly holds 33,481 shares of Duke Energy common stock.

feed.title: Duke Energy (NYSE: DUK) CEO has 2,016 shares withheld for taxes
feed.summary: Duke Energy President and CEO Harry K. Sideris had 2,016 shares of common stock withheld for tax obligations on April 1, 2026. This transaction, valued at $130.90 per share, covered taxes due on the vesting of 4,640 restricted stock units (RSUs) awarded on March 11, 2024. Following this non-market disposition, Sideris directly holds 116,102 shares and an indirect interest in 2,538 shares through a 401(k) fund.

feed.title: A rail merger promise: lifetime job protection for train dispatchers
feed.summary: The American Train Dispatchers Association (ATDA) and Union Pacific Railroad (UNP) have reached an agreement guaranteeing lifetime job protection for union employees if Union Pacific merges with Norfolk Southern. This marks the sixth national union to secure such a deal with Union Pacific, ensuring job security for current employees, subject to continued employment requirements. Union Pacific CEO Jim Vena stated that this agreement underscores the merger's benefits for employees, the nation, and customers.

feed.title: Fortive will webcast its Q1 2026 earnings call on April 30
feed.summary: Fortive Corporation (NYSE: FTV) announced it will webcast its first-quarter 2026 earnings conference call on Thursday, April 30, 2026, at 12:00 p.m. ET. The call will be accessible via their website's "Investors" section and by dialing a provided telephone number. Fortive, headquartered in Everett, Washington, specializes in essential technologies across Intelligent Operating Solutions and Advanced Healthcare Solutions.

feed.title: Wells Fargo backs Meta, Alphabet ahead of earnings despite cuts
feed.summary: Wells Fargo analysts have reaffirmed an overweight rating on Meta Platforms and Alphabet, despite trimming their price targets for both companies. This continued confidence comes as both tech giants face a legal setback and prepare for a closely watched earnings season. The firm projects strong revenue growth for Meta, supported by its AI roadmap, and anticipates accelerated cloud growth for Alphabet, seeing these fundamentals as outweighing recent legal risks and valuation compression.

feed.title: Guarantor: JPMorgan Chase & Co.
feed.summary: This SEC filing (Form 424B2) details JPMorgan Chase Financial Company LLC's offering of $2,504,000 Uncapped Accelerated Barrier Notes linked to the MerQube US Tech+ Vol Advantage Index, fully guaranteed by JPMorgan Chase & Co. The notes are designed for investors seeking an uncapped return based on the Index's appreciation, but come with significant risks including a 6.0% per annum daily deduction on the Index, potential principal loss, and credit risks associated with both JPMorgan Financial and JPMorgan Chase & Co. The document outlines key terms, hypothetical payout profiles, and numerous risk considerations for potential investors.

feed.title: Hasbro hit by cyberattack, investigates possible data breach
feed.summary: Toy giant Hasbro has announced it was hit by a cyberattack on March 28, 2026, which disrupted some company operations. The firm promptly activated its security incident response protocols, took certain systems offline, and launched an investigation with the help of third-party cybersecurity experts. The company is currently working to determine the full scope of the impact and review potentially affected files, and suspects a ransomware group may be responsible, though no group has yet claimed responsibility.

feed.title: Denver Water, Xcel enact plan to ease shortages
feed.summary: Denver Water and Xcel Energy have activated the Shoshone call reduction agreement, allowing Denver Water to divert more water from the Colorado River headwaters due to an abysmal snowpack and spring runoff forecast. This agreement, implemented on March 18th, reduces the call at the Shoshone hydroelectric plant and benefits both the Front Range, by providing more water, and the Western Slope, by allowing reservoirs to fill and allocating 10% of the diverted water to Western Slope entities. The Colorado River Water Conservation District plans to acquire the Shoshone water rights and intends to make these relaxation terms permanent beyond the current agreement's 2032 expiration.

feed.title: How Westinghouse’s Record Backlog and Upbeat Q1 Outlook Could Shape Westinghouse Air Brake (WAB) Investors
feed.summary: Westinghouse Air Brake Technologies (WAB) is set to report its Q1 2026 results, with strong analyst "Outperform" views and optimistic 2026 EPS guidance of US$10.05 to US$10.45. The company's investment narrative is bolstered by a record backlog and consistent earnings outperformance, emphasizing long-term demand for rail modernization and digital solutions. However, investors are cautioned that ongoing weakness in the North American railcar build cycle remains a key risk.

feed.title: Teledyne Deepens Defense And Space Focus With Royal Navy Deal
feed.summary: Teledyne Technologies (NYSE:TDY) has secured a significant contract with the UK Ministry of Defence for autonomous ocean observing systems for the Royal Navy and has reorganized its space operations into a new initiative called Teledyne Space. These moves underscore Teledyne's strategic focus on advanced maritime and space technologies crucial for defense and national security. Investors should monitor how these developments impact contract awards, backlog, and customer exposure, especially considering the stock's recent performance and the company's shift towards complex, long-term defense and space programs.

feed.title: Is AllianceBernstein (AB) Quietly Redefining Its Wealth Strategy With Business Owner Segment Leadership?
feed.summary: AllianceBernstein (AB) is enhancing its wealth management strategy by expanding Kim Mustin's role to lead the Business Owner segment, which advises private business owners on monetizing their companies. This move, alongside the appointment of Onur Erzan as President, aims to counter fee pressure and institutional equity outflows by focusing on high-net-worth client growth and private markets. While new business in this segment is accelerating, investors should remain aware of the potential impact of ongoing institutional equity outflows and fee compression on AllianceBernstein's profitability.

feed.title: Avient (NYSE: AVNT) director adds 1,072 shares via deferred comp plan
feed.summary: Avient Corporation director Kim Ann Mink acquired 1,072 shares of common stock through the company's Deferred Compensation Plan for Non-Employee Directors, including through its dividend reinvestment feature. These shares were credited at no cost. Following this transaction, Mink indirectly holds a total of 37,479.467 Avient shares within the plan.

feed.title: Avient (NYSE: AVNT) director adds 1,072 shares via deferred comp award
feed.summary: Avient (NYSE: AVNT) director William A. Wulfsohn acquired 1,072 shares of common stock on March 31, 2026, as part of a deferred compensation award. These shares were granted at $0.00 per share and are held indirectly through the Avient Corporation Deferred Compensation Plan for Non-Employee Directors. Following this transaction, Wulfsohn's indirect holdings in the plan total 64,043.332 shares, which includes shares acquired via dividend reinvestment.

feed.title: Avient (NYSE: AVNT) director Jellison receives 1,072-share stock award and reports plan holdings
feed.summary: Avient Corp director William R. Jellison received a grant of 1,072 shares of Avient common stock at no cash cost, increasing his direct holdings to 26,125 shares. He also reported indirect holdings of 43,777.215 shares through a Deferred Compensation Plan for Non-Employee Directors, which includes shares from dividend reinvestment. The transaction, indicated by a Form 4 filing, reflects stock-based compensation rather than an open-market purchase.

feed.title: Nicolas Ernest adds 1,072 Avient Corp (AVNT) shares via plan
feed.summary: Avient Corp director Nicolas Ernest has acquired 1,072 shares of Avient common stock on March 31, 2026. These shares were granted as an award through the Avient Corporation Deferred Compensation Plan for Non-Employee Directors, including dividend reinvestment, and were not an open-market purchase. This transaction increases his indirect holdings in the deferred compensation plan to a total of 25,662.568 shares.

feed.title: Avient Corp (AVNT) director granted 1,072 shares in stock award
feed.summary: Avient Corp (AVNT) director Neil Green was granted 1,072 shares of common stock as compensation, increasing his direct holdings to 9,371.007 shares. The shares were awarded at $0.00 per share, indicating a compensation-related issuance rather than an open-market purchase. Green also indirectly holds 8,674.676 shares through the company's Deferred Compensation Plan for Non-Employee Directors, which includes shares accumulated via a dividend reinvestment feature.

feed.title: Norfolk Southern (NYSE: NSC) director receives deferred stock unit grant
feed.summary: Norfolk Southern (NYSE: NSC) director Richard H. Anderson received a grant of 233.4495 deferred stock units as part of his director compensation. These units were valued at $287.00 per share, based on the closing market value on March 31, 2026. Following this grant, Anderson's total deferred stock units in the company's plan now stand at 1,506.4479, which will be settled in cash upon his retirement or at an elected time.

feed.title: Carbon Fiber Bike Frame Maker J-Star’s Founder Returns as CEO
feed.summary: J-Star Holding Co., Ltd., a Taiwanese manufacturer of carbon fiber and composite solutions for rackets and bikes, has reappointed Jonathan Chiang as its chief executive officer. He succeeds Sam Van, who will transition to an advisory role. The Board believes Chiang's return provides experienced leadership during global economic uncertainty and positions the company for future growth, leveraging his deep understanding of the business and its long-term strategy.

feed.title: Brazil's Braskem boosted by petrochemical prices, but debt problem looms
feed.summary: Brazilian petrochemical producer Braskem is benefiting from rising petrochemical prices, which have improved its long-term outlook. However, the company is facing immediate challenges due to upcoming interest payments on its $9.4 billion debt and is considering an injunction to avoid debt restructuring. Delays in a planned management change further complicate its financial situation, though expectations of this change have boosted its share price.

feed.title: Resmed director Farrell sells $450,000 in stock
feed.summary: Peter C. Farrell, a director at ResMed Inc. (NYSE:RMD), sold 2,000 shares of company stock for $450,000 on April 1, 2026, under a Rule 10b5-1 trading plan. The medical device company, which has "GREAT" financial health and has raised its dividend for 14 consecutive years, appears undervalued according to InvestingPro analysis, despite varied recent analyst outlooks. ResMed also reported strong Q2 fiscal 2026 earnings, surpassing EPS expectations.

feed.title: 17 Education & Technology Group Inc (YQ) Shares: Navigating Challenges in China's EdTech Sector for North American Investors
feed.summary: 17 Education & Technology Group Inc (YQ) offers smart in-school classroom solutions and SaaS in China, attracting North American investors interested in its digital transformation potential despite regulatory and market challenges. The company focuses on K-12 education, leveraging AI and data-driven tools, with its revenue derived entirely from China and denominated in Renminbi. YQ's strategy emphasizes SaaS and subscription models, aiming for stability and long-term growth by aligning with government priorities for digital infrastructure in schools.

feed.title: AEP (AEP) director receives phantom stock unit grant tied to shares
feed.summary: Joseph G. Sauvage, a director at AMERICAN ELECTRIC POWER CO INC (AEP), has been granted phantom stock units. This award, tied to 324 underlying shares with a reference price of $131.08 per share, increases his direct holdings to 1,498 phantom stock units. These units will be paid in cash or shares upon the termination of his board service or at a later deferred date, provided payment begins within five years of termination.
